title: Incentivizing Research and Innovation for Salmonella-Resistant Livestock Breeds
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     a     leading     cause     of     foodborne     illness     globally  ,   with     poultry     and     livestock     serving   as   major     reservoirs  .   Traditional     control     measures  —  biosecurity  ,   hygiene  ,   and     vaccination  —  reduce     but     do   not   eliminate     the     risk     of     contamination  .   Advancing     research     toward     developing     Salmonella-resistant     livestock     breeds     offers     a   long-term,   sustainable     solution     to     break     the     transmission     cycle     at     its     source  .   Genetic     selection     and     biotechnological     innovations     can     significantly     lower     pathogen     load   in   animals  ,   reducing     risks     to     public     health     and     economic     losses     due     to     recalls     and     trade     disruptions  .   Governments     can     play     a     pivotal     role     by     providing     incentives  ,   funding  ,   and     policy     frameworks     to     encourage     research     collaborations     between     academia  ,   industry  ,   and     breeders  .   This     approach     strengthens     national     food     safety     systems     and     protects     consumer     health  .
